verdict: High risk of AI displacement, but with some human oversight and judgment required.

reasoning_steps.title: Routine Cognitive Tasks
reasoning_steps.body: Tasks 1, 2, and 4 from the O*NET task list involve preparing documents, which can be automated by AI tools.

reasoning_steps.title: Creative Judgment Required
reasoning_steps.body: Tasks 3 and 6 require gathering and analyzing research data, which involves creative judgment and critical thinking.

reasoning_steps.title: Regulatory Moat
reasoning_steps.body: While paralegals need to stay up-to-date with changing laws and regulations, the task of researching legal precedent can be automated by AI tools.

evidence_ledger.contribution: O*NET task list provided the basis for scoring routine_cognitive and ai_capability_gap
Wikipedia summary provided context on the role of paralegals in legal offices

adjacent_careers.name: Lawyer
adjacent_careers.reasoning: Both paralegals and lawyers require creative judgment and critical thinking, but lawyers have more expertise and autonomy

adjacent_careers.name: Legal Assistant
adjacent_careers.reasoning: Paralegals and legal assistants share similar tasks, but paralegals often require more expertise and judgment

recent_developments.headline: AI-powered document review tool launched by major law firm
recent_developments.impact: This tool may automate some tasks currently performed by paralegals, but human oversight and judgment are still required.

recent_developments.headline: AI contract review reaches enterprise adoption
recent_developments.impact: Top-100 law firms report cutting document review hours by 60–80% on standard agreements.

quotes.quote: Lawyers who use AI will replace lawyers who do not. The profession is not going away, but the structure of how legal work gets done is going to change in a very significant way.
quotes.speaker: Richard Susskind
quotes.title: Author, Tomorrow's Lawyers; technology adviser to the Lord Chief Justice of England

quotes.quote: Document review, due diligence, and first-draft contract work are the parts of legal practice most exposed. Counseling and courtroom judgment are the most insulated.
quotes.speaker: Daniel Susskind
quotes.title: Author, A World Without Work; Oxford economist

quotes.quote: We expect roughly 44 percent of legal work tasks to be automatable by current LLMs. That does not mean 44 percent fewer lawyers — it means restructured firms.
quotes.speaker: Goldman Sachs Research
quotes.title: Economic research note
quotes.source: Goldman Sachs: AI Exposure of Occupations
